titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 599

p.seg: William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting.

Let present and future know me to have approved, and by this my charter to have made firm, the agreement between Prior Bertram and the convent of Durham, and Edward of Old Cambus and his sons, about the abjuration and quitclaim which Edward and his sons made to the said prior and monks of the toun of Old Cambus, and of the mill, and all other things belonging to the toun of Old Cambus, in return for greater Lumsdaine, which the said prior and monks gave to them as an exchange, and in return for money which the said prior and monks acquitted to me for the said Edward. Wherefore I will and instruct that the said agreement about the said abjuration and quiclaim be steadfastly maintained, just as the cyrograph made thereupon between them, and the charter of the said Edward, bear witness. Saving my service.
As witnesses: William and Walter, my chaplains; Philip de Valognes, my chamberlain; William Comyn, William del Bois, and Gilbert, my clerks.
At Haddington on the 8th day of July.
